page: Microsoft Azure EC128eds_v5 is an ECEDV5 family memory-optimized virtual machine instance. It is built on the x86_64 architecture, powered by an Intel processor, and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 128 dedicated vCPUs and 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 6.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server configuration includes 2891 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk attachments. It does not include any integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the EC128eds_v5 offers high resource density, combining a large memory footprint with substantial local storage on dedicated compute resources. This makes the instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, enterprise applications, and data processing tasks that require significant RAM and local disk space without the need for hardware acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure EC128eds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ECEDV5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 Intel processor architecture and is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, yielding 6.0 GB of memory per core. The instance includes 2891 GB of local storage and supports premium storage configurations, though it lacks GPU acceleration and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This high-density hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large relational databases, and extensive data processing workloads that require substantial local disk capacity and dedicated compute resources.
